intro: Leptochilus punctatus, also known as Spiny Wood Fern or Leptochilus punctatus, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate various health issues, including fever reduction, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Leptochilus punctatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Leptochilus punctatus is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in poultices and salves for topical applications.
